mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production indicates expanding economic output, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return frequency in months with INDPRO growth does not exceed 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 8.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses wage-driven inflation and constrains consumer spending, triggering downward revisions to earnings estimates and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 8.0%, or the mean return during triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint weakness in retail sales and housing starts reflects a synchronized demand contraction that amplifies earnings downgrade risk, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 50% of months where both RSAFS and HOUST decline, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) in a two-factor model.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25 percent indicates a zero lower bound constraint that prevents conventional easing, reflects severe economic distress, impairs bank net interest margins, and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 45% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, or the mean triggered return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During high unemployment regimes, accelerating nonfarm payroll growth indicates a recovering labor market that increases household disposable income and consumption expenditures, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of high-unemployment months with rising PAYEMS, or the return spread over low-unemployment months is not positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production level below 98 indicates supply-side constraints that raise input costs and squeeze corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of periods when INDPRO is below 98, or the mean return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and reduces the present value of future cash flows through higher discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC is above -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative return frequency in high CPIAUCSL periods does not exceed 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating growth in real GDP reflects a broad-based expansion in aggregate demand that supports top-line revenue growth across sectors, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when GDPC1 exceeds 13000, or the mean return is not positive (t-test p > 0.05).
